title: Color proportion
lede: Each KIMES sub-brand has a recommended color proportion that defines its visual character. Follow these proportions to keep brand expression consistent across print, digital, and on-site applications.

brandByBrand.title: 04.1 — Brand-by-brand proportions
brandByBrand.desc: Each brand has a recommended split between background, structure, and accent. Bar widths match the percentages exactly.

comparison.title: 04.2 — Comparison view
comparison.desc: Stacked side by side, the proportional differences become obvious — each brand has a distinct visual personality.

applicationMocks.title: 04.3 — Application examples
applicationMocks.desc: How each proportion looks in a real layout. Zones are abstract — the idea is the proportional rhythm, not the content.

acceptableDeviation.title: 04.4 — Acceptable deviation
acceptableDeviation.intro: These proportions are recommendations, not strict ratios. Acceptable deviation is
bullets: Designs that drop the brand color below 10% lose brand recognition.
Designs that exceed 30% feel aggressive and off-brand.

examples.note: Too little — looks generic, no brand presence

donts.title: 04.5 — Don'ts
donts.desc: Four common proportion mistakes. None of these read as on-brand — each breaks the recommended hierarchy in a different way.
